name: RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-5
title: Scope guard override switch (one-time and session-long) not detected or utilized by main session
description: The scope guard hook is designed to support two override modes: one-time (permits a single guarded operation) and session-long (permits all guarded operations for the duration of the session). Neither mode is currently detected from user-granted permission in the conversation, nor is the override applied to allow the guarded Bash operation to proceed. Observed in conversation: user explicitly granted one-time permission twice; the scope guard continued to DENY both times. The workaround was to manually touch .rabbit-scope-active before the guarded command in a separate Bash call.

history.note: Stale: the mechanism is fully implemented. scope-guard.py _consume_override() handles one-time and session modes correctly; .rabbit-scope-override is on the allowlist so Claude can write it; the spec (lines 165-171) and rabbit-feature-touch Override Path both define the confirm-token flow. Bug described absence of mechanism that now exists.
